Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend The Judgment; Declaration Of Ali A. Aalaei
Off Calendar Jul-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Paul Henry's unopposed Motion To Amend The Judgment is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Plaintiff moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 187. "When jurisdiction is, by the Constitution or this Code, or by any other statute, conferred on a Court or judicial officer, all the means necessary to carry it into effect are also given; and in the exercise of this jurisdiction, if the course of proceeding be not specifically pointed out by this Code or the statute, any suitable process or mode of proceeding may be adopted which may appear most conformable to the spirit of this Code." Under section 187, courts have discretion to add parties to a judgment as debtors in the interests of justice, but generally will do so only where the judgment creditor proves the parties to be added are essentially alter egos to the judgment debtors, that is the debtor and the proposed additional debtor are essentially the same entity. This involves demonstrating through evidence a unity of interest and ownership, where the corporate form is disregarded, and the added party exercised control over the original debtor and the litigation. It goes without saying similarity in names is not enough.

By the motion here Plaintiff seeks to add to the judgment as debtors two defendants and change the name of an existing judgment debtor. Plaintiff's evidentiary showing is quite limited. "It has recently been discovered," Counsel testifies, "that Cerego Japan, who was designated in the operative complaint as 'a business entity,' lists itself as a 'corporation,' in a certain manner and style on its documents." (Aalaei Dec. at para. 9.) What specifically was discovered, when and under what circumstances, by whom and how does it establish that "CJKK, a business entity" and "CJKK, a corporation" are essentially the same entity such that it is fair to add the latter as debtor? As to another, Counsel testifies, "It has been discovered that Cerego Japan also does business as Cerego Japan, Inc., which it regards as its 'English name'." (Aalaei Dec. at para. 10.) What specifically was discovered, when and under what circumstances, by whom and how does it establish that CJKK, a business entity and Cerego Japan, Inc. are essentially the same entity such that it is fair to add the latter as debtor? As to Cerego, LLC (see Aalaei Dec. at para. 11), what assets bearing this name were discovered, by whom, when and under what circumstances and how does the evidence establish that Cerego, Inc. and Cerego, LLC are essentially the same entity such that it is fair to add the latter as debtor? Additionally, the court is interested in why Plaintiff did not name Cerego, LLC as a defendant prior to judgment, given that he did name Cerego, LLC as a defendant in the related action (CGC-20-585256). The evidence, of course, must be properly presented.

Additionally, if the parties proposed to be added to the judgment likely have agents for service of process, the parties should be served with any subsequent motion seeking similar relief.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

PLAINTIFF PAUL HENRY'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO SEAL THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED ISO OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE.(FOR TENTATIVE RULING ONLY PART 2 OF 2)
Off Calendar Jul-21-2023 Off Calendar

Motion For An Order To Seal The Evidence Submitted Iso Opposition To Defendant Motion To Quash Service
Off Calendar Jul-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

RE: Plaintiff PAUL HENRY's Motion For An Order To Seal The Evidence Submitted Iso Opposition To Defendant Motion To Quash Service.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied without prejudice. The motion seeks to seal all of (apparently) 18 documents. However, many sections of those documents-perhaps title pages and other sections such as boilerplate provisions of agreements-likely do not contain confidential information. The only facts in support are found in the declaration of E. Young, but that does not appear to address every exhibit, and it does not address all portions of any exhibit. The fact that documents have been marked confidential or produced pursuant to a confidentiality order is not decisive and often immaterial, because parties' agreement to sealing is insufficient to support a sealing order. The definition of material as "confidential" is conclusory and not a basis for sealing: the moving papers must state the harm likely if there should be public disclosure of the material. Any new motion must be heard in Department 611 before Judge Karnow (contact his clerk for a date and time). The moving party must:

- At the time of filing, lodge with Department 611, but not file, a "delta" document which is comprised of documents the subject of the sealing motion, redlined to show the specific words or portions sought to be deleted from the public file;

-Rely only on admissible evidence to show why materials should be sealed, sufficient to support the proposed order noted below;

-At the time of filing, provide paper courtesy copies of all moving papers to Department 611;
-Provide a detailed proposed order in conformity with CRC 2.550 (d) and (e), and 2.551(e), specifically stating the facts that support sealing of every portion to be sealed (boilerplate language just quoting rule of court is alone insufficient).

Frequently, sealing is not needed because the underlying motion does not require the court to inspect the confidential portion of documents. There is no need to provide-and so no need to seal-materials which are irrelevant to the underlying motion. Overstock.com, Inc. v. Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. (2014) 231 Cal.App.4th 471, 500. Thus the court encourages the parties to confer on the materials and if suitable withdraw the materials currently submitted for sealing, and instead simply file in the public file redacted versions of them as necessary for the court's resolution of the underlying motion. The parties may wish to consult the authorities collected in https://works.bepress.com/curtis_karnow/65/.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/CK)
